name: web-development
description: Guide for any work on the python scripts that generate the website, covering how it works
---

# Web Development Guide

## Python Build Script Architecture

The `build.py` script is the core of the website generation system. Understanding its structure is critical for any modifications.

### Core Components

#### 1. DicierPreprocessor Class
- **Purpose**: Preprocesses markdown before conversion to HTML
- **Function**: Converts `{dicier:CODE}` syntax to `<span class="dicier">CODE</span>`
- **Execution Order**: Runs at priority 175 (before markdown processing)
- **Regex Pattern**: `r'\{dicier:([^}]+)\}'` - captures content between braces

#### 2. DicierExtension Class
- **Purpose**: Registers the DicierPreprocessor with the markdown parser
- **Integration**: Adds custom preprocessing to the markdown pipeline

#### 3. generate_cache_buster()
- **Purpose**: Creates unique hash for CSS versioning to prevent browser caching
- **Method**: MD5 hash of current timestamp (first 8 characters)
- **Usage**: Appended to CSS file URLs as query parameter

#### 4. fix_toc_with_dicier()
- **Purpose**: Ensures Dicier spans appear in table of contents
- **Problem Solved**: Markdown TOC extension strips HTML from headers by default
- **Method**: 
  - Extracts headers with IDs from generated HTML using regex
  - Finds matching TOC links
  - Replaces stripped text with full HTML content (including spans)
- **Pattern**: Uses DOTALL flag for multi-line headers

#### 5. add_decorative_headers()
- **Purpose**: Adds decorative SVG images after H1 and H2 elements
- **Assets**: 
  - `/assets/DecorativeHeaderLarge.svg` after H1
  - `/assets/DecorativeHeaderSmall.svg` after H2
- **Accessibility**: Images have empty alt text and `aria-hidden="true"`

#### 6. build_html()
- **Purpose**: Main build orchestration function
- **Process Flow**:
  1. Read `content.md`
  2. Initialize markdown parser with extensions
  3. Convert markdown to HTML and generate TOC
  4. Fix TOC to include Dicier spans
  5. Add decorative headers
  6. Generate cache buster
  7. Read `template.html`
  8. Replace placeholders: `{cache_buster}`, `{toc_html}`, `{html_content}`
  9. Write `index.html`

## Template HTML Structure

### template.html File

The `template.html` file is the skeleton HTML that `build.py` populates with generated content. It defines the entire page structure, layout, and interactive behavior.

#### Template Placeholders

Three placeholders are replaced during build:

1. **`{cache_buster}`** - Inserted into CSS link as query parameter
   - Location: `<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css?v={cache_buster}">`
   - Ensures browsers don't cache old CSS versions

2. **`{toc_html}`** - Table of contents HTML inserted into sidebar
   - Location: Inside `<nav class="sidebar">`
   - Generated by markdown `toc` extension
   - Fixed by `fix_toc_with_dicier()` to preserve Dicier spans

3. **`{html_content}`** - Main content from markdown conversion
   - Location: Inside `<div class="content-wrapper">`
   - Contains all generated HTML from `content.md`
   - Includes Dicier spans and decorative headers

#### Key Template Sections

**1. Head Section**
```html
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <meta name="description" content="Dichotomy">
    <title>Dichotomy</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css?v={cache_buster}">
</head>
```
- Character encoding for Unicode support
- Viewport meta for responsive design
- Cache-busted stylesheet link

**2. Skip to Content Link**
```html
<a href="#main-content" class="skip-to-content">Skip to main content</a>
```
- First tabbable element for accessibility
- Hidden until focused (CSS `.skip-to-content`)
- Navigates to `#main-content`

**3. Burger Menu Button**
```html
<button class="burger-menu" id="burgerMenu" aria-label="Toggle menu">
    <span></span>
    <span></span>
    <span></span>
</button>
```
- Appears on mobile (≤768px)
- ARIA label for accessibility
- Three spans create hamburger icon

**4. Sidebar Navigation**
```html
<nav class="sidebar" id="sidebar">
    <div class="sidebar-header">
        <h1>Table of Contents</h1>
    </div>
    {toc_html}
</nav>
```
- Semantic `<nav>` element
- TOC placeholder replaced with generated content
- Toggles visibility via JavaScript on mobile

**5. Main Content Area**
```html
<main class="content" id="main-content">
    <div class="content-wrapper">
        {html_content}
    </div>
    <footer class="site-footer">
        <!-- License and attribution -->
    </footer>
</main>
```
- Semantic `<main>` with ID for skip link
- Content wrapper for styling
- Footer with license info

**6. Footer License Section**
```html
<footer class="site-footer">
    <div class="footer-section">
        <h3>License</h3>
        <p>CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 with links</p>
    </div>
    <div class="footer-section">
        <h3>Font Attribution</h3>
        <p>Dicier font CC BY 4.0 with links</p>
    </div>
</footer>
```
- Displays Dichotomy license (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0)
- Displays Dicier font license (CC BY 4.0)
- Links to full license texts

**7. JavaScript for Interactivity**
```javascript
// Toggle sidebar on mobile
burgerMenu.addEventListener('click', () => {
    sidebar.classList.toggle('active');
    burgerMenu.classList.toggle('active');
});

// Close sidebar when clicking TOC links (mobile)
// Close sidebar when clicking outside (mobile)
```
- Burger menu toggle functionality
- Auto-close sidebar on mobile after navigation
- Click-outside-to-close on mobile

#### Modifying template.html

**When to modify:**
- Adding new static HTML sections
- Changing page structure or layout
- Adding new metadata or links
- Modifying footer content
- Adding new interactive elements

**After modifying:**
1. Run `python3 build.py` to regenerate `index.html`
2. Test all interactive features (burger menu, skip link, TOC links)
3. Verify accessibility (screen reader, keyboard nav)
4. Test responsive behavior at all breakpoints
5. Update this skill file if changes are significant

### Markdown Extensions Used

```python
md = markdown.Markdown(extensions=[
    'toc',              # Table of contents generation
    'extra',            # Tables, code blocks, fenced code, etc.
    'nl2br',            # Convert newlines to <br> tags
    DicierExtension(),  # Custom Dicier font support
])
```

## Python Style Rules

### Code Organization
- Keep functions focused on single responsibility
- Document complex regex patterns with inline comments
- Use descriptive function and variable names

### File Handling
- Always use `with` statements for file operations
- Specify encoding explicitly: `encoding='utf-8'`
- Close files properly (automatic with `with`)

### Docstrings
- All functions must have docstrings
- Explain the "why" not just the "what"
- Document complex logic or non-obvious behavior

### Regular Expressions
- Use raw strings for regex patterns: `r'pattern'`
- Comment complex patterns explaining each part
- Use named groups for clarity when applicable: `(?P<name>...)`

## License Headers

**CRITICAL**: All Python files must include this license header:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build script to convert markdown to HTML with table of contents.

This file is part of Dichotomy.
This work is licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.
License: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SteffenBlake/Dichotomy/refs/heads/main/LICENSE
"""
```

## After Making Changes

### Required Steps

1. **Test Locally**
   - Run `python3 build.py` to verify script executes without errors
   - Check generated `index.html` for correct output
   - Verify Dicier codes render correctly
   - Test TOC links work properly

2. **Check GitHub Deployment**
   - After pushing changes, navigate to repository Actions tab
   - Verify the deployment workflow succeeds
   - Visit the live site at `https://steffenblake.github.io/Dichotomy/`
   - Test all functionality on the deployed site

3. **Update Skill Files**
   - If you change how the build script works, update this skill file
   - If you add new Dicier features, update `dicier-user-guide/SKILL.md`
   - If you change markdown processing, update `markdown-editting/SKILL.md`
   - Document any new troubleshooting steps in `testing-debugging/SKILL.md`

### GitHub Actions Workflow

The `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` file handles automatic deployment:
- Triggers on push to `main` branch
- Sets up Python 3.11
- Installs dependencies from `requirements.txt`
- Runs `build.py`
- Deploys to GitHub Pages

**If you modify the build process, ensure the workflow file remains compatible.**

## Common Modification Patterns

### Adding New Markdown Extensions
```python
md = markdown.Markdown(extensions=[
    'toc',
    'extra',
    'nl2br',
    DicierExtension(),
    YourNewExtension(),  # Add here
])
```

### Adding New Preprocessors
```python
class YourPreprocessor(Preprocessor):
    def run(self, lines):
        # Process lines
        return modified_lines

class YourExtension(Extension):
    def extendMarkdown(self, md):
        md.preprocessors.register(YourPreprocessor(md), 'yourname', priority)
```

### Adding Template Variables
1. Add placeholder in `template.html`: `{your_variable}`
2. Generate value in `build_html()`
3. Add to `.format()` call: `your_variable=value`

## HTML Generation Requirements

### Accessibility Standards

**CRITICAL**: Accessibility for screen readers must be maintained at all times. The Python code and template must generate HTML that follows proper modern standards.

#### Required Accessibility Features

1. **Skip to Content Link**
   - The very first tabbable element must be a "Skip to main content" link
   - Must be hidden until focused (CSS: `.skip-to-content`)
   - Must link to `#main-content`
   - Already implemented in `template.html`

2. **Semantic HTML**
   - Use proper heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3, no skipping levels)
   - Use `<nav>` for navigation areas
   - Use `<main>` for main content
   - Use `<footer>` for footer content

3. **Alt Text**
   - All images must have appropriate alt text
   - Decorative images must use empty alt (`alt=""`) with `aria-hidden="true"`
   - Example: Decorative headers use `alt=""` and `aria-hidden="true"`

4. **ARIA Labels**
   - Interactive elements must have proper ARIA labels
   - Burger menu button has `aria-label="Toggle menu"`
   - Hidden content uses `aria-hidden="true"`

5. **Keyboard Navigation**
   - All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible
   - Proper tab order maintained
   - Focus states visible

### Mobile-Friendly Requirements

**CRITICAL**: The website must be designed to look very good on mobile devices.

#### Responsive Design Requirements

1. **Viewport Meta Tag**
   - Must include: `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">`
   - Already in `template.html`

2. **Mobile Navigation**
   - Burger menu appears on screens ≤768px
   - Sidebar hidden by default on mobile
   - Sidebar slides in when burger menu clicked
   - Sidebar closes when clicking outside or on a link

3. **Responsive Breakpoints**
   - Mobile: ≤768px
   - Tablet: >768px and <1024px
   - Desktop: ≥1024px

4. **Touch Targets**
   - Minimum 44×44px touch targets for mobile
   - Adequate spacing between interactive elements

5. **Content Reflow**
   - Content must be readable without horizontal scrolling
   - Images and tables must scale appropriately
   - Text must remain legible at mobile sizes

### License Display

The website footer must visually display the license information:
- CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 license linked properly
- Link to full license text at GitHub
- Dicier font attribution (CC BY 4.0)
- Already implemented in `template.html` footer
